perturbation.original_text: 5. Intellectual Property (a) Nothing in this Agreement shall be cons trued as granting or conferring any rights, interests or intellectual property right s by assignment, license or otherwise, relating to any Confidential Information in or for any intellectual property, invention, discovery or improvement or derivative made, conceived or acquired prior or subsequent to the Effective Date. Except for the limited right to use the Confidential Information as set forth above, all intellectual property, patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret and a ll other rights and interests in the Confidential Information and the products, inventions, ideas, works, creation, symbols, data, reports, opinions and all other concepts and materials developed, invented, reduced to practice, authored or created by Receiver (including portions and derivatives thereof) in any medi um arising from or relating to this Agreement (“ Intellectual Property ”) shall be the sole and exclusive property of VIDAR. (b) All Intellectual Property that are origi nal works of authorship shall be considered works made for hire (as that phrase is used in Section 101 of the United States Copyright Act, 17 U.S.C. § 101 or analogous la w). If any Intellectual Property is found not to be a work made for hire and/or is owned by Receiver or any third party, Receiver hereby irrevocably (and shall cause any third party owner to) assigns and transfers all rights to and inte rests in such Intellectual Property to VIDAR without further consideration. VIDAR Confidential Page 2 of 4 18268-001 Rev B (c) Receiver shall promptly disclose a ll Intellectual Property to VIDAR and shall make and maintain adequate and current written records of all Intellectual Property. Receiver agrees to take all actions, execute all instruments and fully cooperate with VIDAR, its successors, assigns or nominees in which, in the opinion of VIDAR, are expedient or necessa ry at any time to file, prosecute, register, evidence, secure, maintain or lit igate all such VIDAR rights and interests in the Intellectual Property throughout the wo rld, including all commercial, legal or administrative proceedings involving the same. All expenses of applying for and obtaining such rights and interests sha ll be borne by VIDAR. VIDAR shall reimburse Receiver for pre-approved expenses paid in connection with such cooperation.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Intellectual Property (a) Nothing in this Agreement shall be cons trued as granting or conferring any rights, interests or intellectual property right s by assignment, license or otherwise, relating to any Confidential Information in or for any intellectual property, invention, discovery or improvement or derivative made, conceived or acquired prior or subsequent to the Effective Date. Unless explicitly agreed otherwise in writing, Receiver retains ownership of all Intellectual Property conceived or developed independently by Receiver using its own resources, even if related to the Confidential Information. (b) All Intellectual Property that are origi nal works of authorship shall be considered works made for hire (as that phrase is used in Section 101 of the United States Copyright Act, 17 U.S.C. § 101 or analogous la w). If any Intellectual Property is found not to be a work made for hire and/or is owned by Receiver or any third party, Receiver hereby irrevocably (and shall cause any third party owner to) assigns and transfers all rights to and inte rests in such Intellectual Property to VIDAR without further consideration. VIDAR Confidential Page 2 of 4 18268-001 Rev B (c) Receiver shall promptly disclose a ll Intellectual Property to VIDAR and shall make and maintain adequate and current written records of all Intellectual Property. Receiver agrees to take all actions, execute all instruments and fully cooperate with VIDAR, its successors, assigns or nominees in which, in the opinion of VIDAR, are expedient or necessa ry at any time to file, prosecute, register, evidence, secure, maintain or lit igate all such VIDAR rights and interests in the Intellectual Property throughout the wo rld, including all commercial, legal or administrative proceedings involving the same. All expenses of applying for and obtaining such rights and interests sha ll be borne by VIDAR. VIDAR shall reimburse Receiver for pre-approved expenses paid in connection with such cooperation.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that all Intellectual Property related to the Agreement shall be the sole and exclusive property of VIDAR. The modified clause allows the Receiver to retain ownership of Intellectual Property developed independently using its own resources. This introduces a contradiction regarding IP ownership. It creates an in-text contradiction in Section 5(c), which requires the Receiver to disclose all Intellectual Property to VIDAR and cooperate with VIDAR in securing rights to it, including Intellectual property developed independently by Receiver.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Receiver shall promptly disclose a ll Intellectual Property to VIDAR and shall make and maintain adequate and current written records of all Intellectual Property. Receiver agrees to take all actions, execute all instruments and fully cooperate with VIDAR, its successors, assigns or nominees in which, in the opinion of VIDAR, are expedient or necessa ry at any time to file, prosecute, register, evidence, secure, maintain or lit igate all such VIDAR rights and interests in the Intellectual Property throughout the wo rld, including all commercial, legal or administrative proceedings involving the same. All expenses of applying for and obtaining such rights and interests sha ll be borne by VIDAR. VIDAR shall reimburse Receiver for pre-approved expenses paid in connection with such cooperation.
perturbation.justification: The original text gives VIDAR sole and exclusive property of all Intellectual Property related to the agreement. The changed text allows Receiver to retain ownership of Intellectual Property developed independently using its own resources, unless explicitly agreed otherwise in writing. This creates a contradiction with section (c), which requires the Receiver to disclose all Intellectual Property to VIDAR, even independently developed IP. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding IP ownership and disclosure obligations and therefore makes the agreement internally inconsistent and likely unenforceable as written.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Return of Confidential Information. U pon the request of VIDAR or the termination or expiration of this Confidential Agreement, Re ceiver shall promptly return to VIDAR all copies of the Confidential Information and obtained by Receiver.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Return of Confidential Information. Upon the request of VIDAR or the termination of this Agreement, Receiver shall return all copies of Confidential Information, unless such return is deemed impractical due to the Receiver's internal archiving policies, in which case the Receiver may retain such copies securely and confidentially.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates the prompt return of all Confidential Information upon VIDAR's request or termination of the agreement. The modified clause allows for exceptions based on the Receiver's internal archiving policies, creating a contradiction. This contradicts Section 8, because VIDAR derives economic value from the Confidential Information not being generally known to other persons who can obtain economic value from its disclosure or use; so the return of the confidential information is very important.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Injunction. Receiver acknowledges and agrees that: (i) the Confidential Information is proprietary to and valuable information of VIDAR; (ii) VIDAR derives economic value from the Confidential Information not being generally known to other persons who can obtain economic value from its discl osure or use; (iii) any disclosure or unauthorized use of the Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm and loss to VIDAR; and (iv) if this Agr eement is breached, VIDAR can not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, VIDAR, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent further breaches of this Agreem ent and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. Receiver shall reimburse VIDAR for all costs and expenses, including attorney’s fees, incurr ed by VIDAR in enforcing the obligations of Receiver.
perturbation.justification: The modified clause in Section 6 introduces an exception to the prompt return of Confidential Information based on the Receiver's internal archiving policies. This directly contradicts Section 8, which emphasizes the economic value VIDAR derives from the confidentiality of the information and the irreparable harm caused by its disclosure. The exception in Section 6 allows for retention, potentially leading to disclosure, which undermines the core premise of Section 8 regarding the necessity of maintaining strict confidentiality to protect VIDAR's economic interests. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Disclaimer. Receiver acknowledges that the Confidential Information may relate to processes or products that are devel opmental and may or may not have been reduced to practical application in prot otype form. Accordingly, Receiver understands that neither VIDAR has made or makes any representation or warranty as to the accuracy, completeness or va lue of the Confidential Information. RECEIVER AGREES THAT NEITHER VIDAR NOR ANY OF ITS REPRESENTATIVES SHALL HAVE ANY LIABILI TY TO RECEIVER OR ANY OF ITS REPRESENTATIVES RESULTING FROM RECEIVER’S ACCESS TO OR USE OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Disclaimer. While Receiver acknowledges that the Confidential Information may relate to developmental processes or products, VIDAR warrants that all Confidential Information provided is accurate and complete to the best of its knowledge at the time of disclosure. VIDAR shall not be liable for Receiver’s use of Confidential Information except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct by VIDAR.
perturbation.explanation: The original disclaimer provides that VIDAR makes no warranties regarding the Confidential Information. The modified version introduces a warranty of accuracy and completeness, and limits VIDAR's liability to cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, directly contradicting the original 'no liability' clause. This creates an in-text contradiction with the last sentences of the original Section 7, where RECEIVER AGREES THAT NEITHER VIDAR NOR ANY OF ITS REPRESENTATIVES SHALL HAVE ANY LIABILITY TO RECEIVER OR ANY OF ITS REPRESENTATIVES RESULTING FROM RECEIVER’S ACCESS TO OR USE OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Disclaimer. Receiver acknowledges that the Confidential Information may relate to processes or products that are devel opmental and may or may not have been reduced to practical application in prot otype form. Accordingly, Receiver understands that neither VIDAR has made or makes any representation or warranty as to the accuracy, completeness or va lue of the Confidential Information. RECEIVER AGREES THAT NEITHER VIDAR NOR ANY OF ITS REPRESENTATIVES SHALL HAVE ANY LIABILI TY TO RECEIVER OR ANY OF ITS REPRESENTATIVES RESULTING FROM RECEIVER’S ACCESS TO OR USE OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that VIDAR makes no warranties and has no liability. The changed text introduces a warranty of accuracy and completeness and limits liability to gross negligence or willful misconduct. This directly contradicts the original disclaimer of no liability, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The Contradicted Text is also the original text which contains the no liability clause. This contradicts with the Changed Text warranty of accuracy and completeness.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Injunction. Receiver acknowledges and agrees that: (i) the Confidential Information is proprietary to and valuable information of VIDAR; (ii) VIDAR derives economic value from the Confidential Information not being generally known to other persons who can obtain economic value from its discl osure or use; (iii) any disclosure or unauthorized use of the Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm and loss to VIDAR; and (iv) if this Agr eement is breached, VIDAR can not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, VIDAR, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent further breaches of this Agreem ent and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. Receiver shall reimburse VIDAR for all costs and expenses, including attorney’s fees, incurr ed by VIDAR in enforcing the obligations of Receiver.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Injunction. Receiver acknowledges and agrees that: (i) the Confidential Information is proprietary to and valuable information of VIDAR; (ii) VIDAR derives economic value from the Confidential Information not being generally known to other persons who can obtain economic value from its discl osure or use; (iii) any disclosure or unauthorized use of the Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm and loss to VIDAR; and (iv) if this Agreement is breached, VIDAR can not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, VIDAR's entitlement to an injunction is limited to cases where the Confidential Information is deemed a "trade secret" under applicable law. In all other cases, VIDAR's sole remedy for breach shall be monetary damages. Receiver shall only reimburse VIDAR for reasonable attorney's fees.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows VIDAR to seek an injunction for any breach of the agreement. The modified version limits the availability of injunctions to cases involving 'trade secrets,' making monetary damages the sole remedy for other breaches. This introduces a contradiction by limiting the remedies available to VIDAR for breaches not involving trade secrets, affecting the entire section's enforceability and potentially conflicting with clause (iv). This creates an in-text contradiction in the sentence where 'Receiver shall reimburse VIDAR for all costs and expenses, including attorney’s fees, incurr ed by VIDAR in enforcing the obligations of Receiver.', because now it says 'Receiver shall only reimburse VIDAR for reasonable attorney's fees.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Injunction. Receiver acknowledges and agrees that: (i) the Confidential Information is proprietary to and valuable information of VIDAR; (ii) VIDAR derives economic value from the Confidential Information not being generally known to other persons who can obtain economic value from its discl osure or use; (iii) any disclosure or unauthorized use of the Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm and loss to VIDAR; and (iv) if this Agr eement is breached, VIDAR can not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, VIDAR, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent further breaches of this Agreem ent and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. Receiver shall reimburse VIDAR for all costs and expenses, including attorney’s fees, incurr ed by VIDAR in enforcing the obligations of Receiver.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits VIDAR's entitlement to an injunction to cases involving "trade secrets," while the original clause and contradicted text state that VIDAR is entitled to an injunction for any breach of the agreement. This introduces a clear and direct conflict regarding the remedies available to VIDAR, directly contradicting the contradicted text. The altered reimbursement clause ('Receiver shall only reimburse VIDAR for reasonable attorney's fees.') directly contradicts the original clause ('Receiver shall reimburse VIDAR for all costs and expenses, including attorney’s fees').

perturbation.original_text: 9. General. This Agreement shall be gov erned by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia and all actions involving this Agreement shall be brought in the state or federal courts located in the Commonwealth of Virginia, to which the parties consent to jurisdiction. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties, their legal representatives, successors and assigns. Except as otherwise expressly provided herein, neither this Agreement, nor any rights granted hereunder may be assigned, transferred, conveyed or encumbered, whether voluntarily or by operation of law, by Receiver without the prior written consent of VI DAR (which may be granted or withheld in VIDAR’s sole and absolute judgment), and any attempt to do so will be deemed null and void. This Agreement becomes effectiv e on the Effective Date and continues in effect thereafter for so long as any Conf idential Information continues to exist. VIDAR may terminate this Agreement at any time for any reason. Any provision of this Agreement which is prohibited, unenforceable or not authorized in any jurisdiction shall, as to such jurisdicti on, be ineffective to the extent of such prohibition, unenforceability or non-authorization without in validating the remaining VIDAR Confidential Page 3 of 4 18268-001 Rev B VIDAR Confidential Page 4 of 4 18268-001 Rev B provisions hereof or affecting the validity, enforceability or legality of such provision in any other jurisdiction. This Ag reement contains t he entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter contained herein, supersedes all prior oral or written understandings and agreements relating thereto, except as expressly otherwise provided, and may not be altered, modified or waived in whole or in part, except in writing, si gned by duly authorized representatives of the parties hereto. The failure of either par ty to insist upon the performance of any of the terms, covenants, conditions or provis ions of this Agreement shall not be considered a waiver or relinquishment of future compliance therewith; nor shall a waiver by either party of any breach or any term, covenant, condition, agreement or provision, operate as a waiver of any ot her term, covenant condition, agreement or provision. This Agreement may be execut ed in any number of counterparts and by different parties hereto in separate count erparts, each of which when so executed shall be deemed to be an original and all of which when taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, this Agreement has been executed by the following duly authorized representatives of the parties: VIDAR SYSTEMS CORPORATION RECEIVER By: _____________________________ Signature By: _____________________________ Signature Print: _____________________________ Print: _____________________________ Title: _____________________________ Title: _____________________________ Date: _____________________________ Date: _____________________________
perturbation.changed_text: 9. General. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the state where the Receiver is headquartered, and all actions involving this Agreement shall be brought in the courts of that state, notwithstanding the initial paragraph. This Agreement shall be binding. Except as otherwise expressly provided herein, neither this Agreement, nor any rights granted hereunder may be assigned, transferred, conveyed or encumbered, whether voluntarily or by operation of law, by Receiver without the prior written consent of VI DAR (which may be granted or withheld in VIDAR’s sole and absolute judgment), and any attempt to do so will be deemed null and void. VIDAR may only terminate this agreement if the Receiver is found to be in breach of any terms or conditions outlined in this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies Virginia law and courts for dispute resolution. The modified version changes the governing law and jurisdiction to the Receiver's headquarters' state, contradicting the initial paragraph, and limits VIDAR's termination rights. The first sentences in the original and modified clause causes this contradiction.

perturbation.justification: The original clause specifies Virginia law and courts for dispute resolution. The modified version changes the governing law and jurisdiction to the Receiver's headquarters' state. This directly contradicts the initial paragraph (which also serves as the Contradicted Text). The modified text also limits VIDAR's termination rights, while the original allowed termination 'at any time for any reason'. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty, making it a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.
